event: Saint Ambrose forbids Emperor Theodosius from entering the cathedral
symbols: bishop's mitre and crosier — spiritual authority that confronts temporal power
emperor's red cloak and armor — worldly military might humbled before the Church
cathedral doorway with columns — the threshold of sacred space from which the unrepentant are barred
bishop's outstretched hand — the gesture of prohibition and prophetic rebuke
soldiers behind the emperor — the force of empire rendered powerless before moral authority
description: A dramatic confrontation scene before a cathedral entrance with massive stone columns. On the right, a bishop in an ornate black and gold vestment with a tall golden mitre raises his hand in a commanding gesture of prohibition, holding a golden crosier. Facing him from the left, a man in Roman military dress with a red cloak and armor leans forward with an imploring or surprised expression. Behind the military figure, soldiers with spears and attendants press forward. Behind the bishop, clergy in white robes observe.

_meta.title: Emperor Theodosius Forbidden by St Ambrose To Enter Milan Cathedral
